data.content:
Here's my analysis of all the legacy patterns found:

| # | Legacy Pattern | Modernization |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="...">` | → `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` |
| 2 | Manual `<Import>` of Common.props / CSharp.targets | → Removed (SDK handles it) |
| 3 | Explicit `Configuration`/`Platform` conditionals | → Removed (SDK defaults) |
| 4 | `<ProjectGuid>` | → Removed (not needed) |
| 5 | `<TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2` | → `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>` |
| 6 | `<FileAlignment>`, `<Deterministic>` | → Removed (SDK defaults) |
| 7 | Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks | → Removed (SDK defaults) |
| 8 | Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | → Removed (implicit for net472) |
| 9 | Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | → Removed (SDK auto-globs `*.cs`) |
| 10 | `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | → Moved to `<PropertyGroup>`, file deleted |
| 11 | `<RootNamespace>`/`<AssemblyName>` matching project name | → Removed (inferred from filename) |
| 12 | `<AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>` | → Removed (default for library) |

Now applying the changes:

arguments.old_str:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props" Condition="Exists('$(MSBuildExtensionsPath)\$(MSBuildToolsVersion)\Microsoft.Common.props')" />
  <PropertyGroup>
    <Configuration Condition=" '$(Configuration)' == '' ">Debug</Configuration>
    <Platform Condition=" '$(Platform)' == '' ">AnyCPU</Platform>
    <ProjectGuid>{12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012}</ProjectGuid>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <RootNamespace>LegacyApp</RootNamespace>
    <AssemblyName>LegacyApp</AssemblyName>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    <FileAlignment>512</FileAlignment>
    <AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>true</AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>
    <Deterministic>true</Deterministic>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Debug|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
    <DebugType>full</DebugType>
    <Optimize>false</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Debug\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>DEBUG;TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <PropertyGroup Condition=" '$(Configuration)|$(Platform)' == 'Release|AnyCPU' ">
    <DebugType>pdbonly</DebugType>
    <Optimize>true</Optimize>
    <OutputPath>bin\Release\</OutputPath>
    <DefineConstants>TRACE</DefineConstants>
    <ErrorReport>prompt</ErrorReport>
    <WarningLevel>4</WarningLevel>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Reference Include="System" />
    <Reference Include="System.Core" />
    <Reference Include="System.Data" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml" />
    <Reference Include="System.Xml.Linq" />
    <Reference Include="Microsoft.CSharp" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="Calculator.cs" />
    <Compile Include="StringHelper.cs" />
    <Compile Include="Properties\AssemblyInfo.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <Import Project="$(MSBuildToolsPath)\Microsoft.CSharp.targets" />
</Project>
arguments.new_str:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>
    <Description>A legacy-style project for modernization testing</Description>
    <Company>TestCo</Company>
    <Copyright>Copyright © 2024</Copyright>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and explain how to modernize them.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and XML namespace — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Manual `<Import>` of Microsoft.Common.props and Microsoft.CSharp.targets** — SDK-style handles this automatically
3. **Explicit `<Configuration>` and `<Platform>` properties** — SDK-style has sensible defaults
4. **`<ProjectGuid>`** — not needed in SDK-style


5. **`<TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.7.2`** — becomes `<TargetFramework>net472</TargetFramework>`
6. **`<FileAlignment>`, `<AutoGenerateBindingRedirects>`, and `<Deterministic>`** — all defaults in SDK-style
7. **Debug/Release PropertyGroups** — SDK-style provides sensible defaults for debug symbols, optimization levels, output paths, and warning levels, so these can be removed entirely
8. **System assembly references, explicit `<Compile>` items, and `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — all handled automatically through implicit globbing and auto-generated attributes in SDK-style
9. **`<OutputType>`, `<RootNamespace>`, and `<AssemblyName>`** — can be omitted since they match the project defaults

Now I'm converting this to a modern SDK-style project file...

Writing modernized .csproj...

With the assembly metadata moved into the project properties, I can safely delete the AssemblyInfo.cs file since those attributes are now auto-generated.

data.content:
**Done.** The project went from 47 lines down to 11. Here's a summary of what changed:

- **Project root** → SDK-style (`Microsoft.NET.Sdk`), removing manual imports
- **Framework** → `net472` TFM moniker replaces `TargetFrameworkVersion`
- **Debug/Release configs** → Removed; SDK defaults handle `Optimize`, `DefineConstants`, `OutputPath`, etc.
- **System references** → Removed; implicit for .NET Framework in SDK-style
- **Compile items** → Removed; SDK auto-globs all `*.cs` files
- **AssemblyInfo.cs** → Deleted; metadata (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`) moved into `<PropertyGroup>` where the SDK auto-generates assembly attributes
- **Boilerplate** (`ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic`, `ErrorReport`, `WarningLevel`) → Removed as SDK defaults
